classDescription: A disciplined martial artist who fights with unarmed techniques, monk weapons, and Focus-powered mobility and defense.
roleIdentity: Mobile striker-controller with unarmed/monk-weapon tempo, Focus-fueled bonus actions, and reactive defenses.

monk-martial-arts.name: Martial Arts
monk-martial-arts.summary: Use Dexterity for unarmed/monk-weapon attacks, scale unarmed damage with your Martial Arts die, and unlock bonus-action unarmed follow-ups.
monk-martial-arts.description:
Your unarmed strikes and monk weapons become one combat engine. You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for attack and damage rolls with unarmed strikes and monk weapons, and your unarmed damage uses your Martial Arts die.

When you take the Attack action with an unarmed strike or monk weapon, you can make one unarmed strike as a bonus action.
monk-martial-arts.effect: Enables Dexterity-based monk-weapon/unarmed attacks and bonus-action unarmed follow-up after Attack.

monk-unarmored-defense.name: Unarmored Defense
monk-unarmored-defense.summary: Your Armor Class comes from your body and stats instead of needing armor.
monk-unarmored-defense.description:
This feature replaces the default armor math with a class-specific formula, letting your build stay effective even when you are not wearing armor. It matters every time your Dexterity, Constitution, or Wisdom changes depending on class.

In play, this is passive but important: the sheet should recalculate AC correctly and keep that value stable across rests, reloads, and equipment changes.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-weapon-mastery-2-weapons.name: Weapon Mastery (2 weapons)
monk-weapon-mastery-2-weapons.summary: You unlock weapon rider properties that make attacks do more than just deal damage.
monk-weapon-mastery-2-weapons.description:
Weapon Mastery gives trained weapons additional tactical texture. The important thing is not just knowing you have mastery, but understanding which weapons can trigger which rider and when that matters in a fight.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-flurry-of-blows.name: Flurry of Blows
monk-flurry-of-blows.summary: Spend focus to convert momentum into extra strikes.
monk-flurry-of-blows.description: After taking the Attack action, you can spend 1 Focus Point to make two unarmed strikes as a bonus action.
monk-flurry-of-blows.usage: Spend 1 Focus Point after the Attack action.
monk-flurry-of-blows.effect: Make two unarmed strikes as a bonus action.

monk-patient-defense.name: Patient Defense
monk-patient-defense.summary: Spend focus to become much harder to pin down or hit cleanly.
monk-patient-defense.description: Spend 1 Focus Point as a bonus action to enter a defensive stance. You gain Disengage and enemies have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you until the start of your next turn.
monk-patient-defense.usage: Spend 1 Focus Point as a bonus action.
monk-patient-defense.effect: Take the Disengage action and gain Disadvantage on attack rolls against you until the start of your next turn.

monk-step-of-the-wind.name: Step of the Wind
monk-step-of-the-wind.summary: Spend focus to move like a problem the battlefield cannot easily hold.
monk-step-of-the-wind.description: Spend 1 Focus Point to take both Dash and Disengage as a bonus action, and your jump distance is doubled for the turn.
monk-step-of-the-wind.usage: Spend 1 Focus Point as a bonus action.
monk-step-of-the-wind.effect: Take Dash and Disengage as a bonus action; jump distance doubles for the turn.

monk-monastic-tradition-subclass.name: Monastic Tradition (Subclass)
monk-monastic-tradition-subclass.summary: Choose your tradition and unlock a stronger identity for the rest of the build.
monk-monastic-tradition-subclass.description: This is the subclass gate for the monk. Once chosen, your tradition should drive later feature cards, action buttons, resource hooks, and playstyle expectations instead of sitting as a hidden background choice.

monk-deflect-attacks.name: Deflect Attacks
monk-deflect-attacks.summary: Use your reaction to blunt or redirect incoming punishment.
monk-deflect-attacks.description:
Deflect Attacks is a reactive defense that should feel immediate and tactical. When it is available, the player should know they have a real answer to ranged or weapon pressure instead of eating every hit full-force.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.
monk-deflect-attacks.trigger: When a visible creature hits you with an attack roll.
monk-deflect-attacks.usage: 1 reaction; optional 1 Focus Point to redirect.
monk-deflect-attacks.effect: Use your reaction to reduce the attack damage by 1d10 + Dexterity modifier + Monk level. If reduced to 0, you can spend 1 Focus Point to redirect the attack.

monk-ability-score-improvement.name: Ability Score Improvement
monk-ability-score-improvement.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
monk-ability-score-improvement.description:
Ability Score Improvement is one of the biggest character-shaping progression points. It can either raise the numbers your class depends on most or open a feat that changes what the character can do.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-slow-fall.name: Slow Fall
monk-slow-fall.summary: Reduce fall damage and turn dangerous height into a manageable problem.
monk-slow-fall.description:
Slow Fall is situational, but when it matters it matters a lot. The feature is there to make vertical maps and risky traversal feel like monk territory.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.
monk-slow-fall.effect: Use your reaction to reduce falling damage by 5 × your Monk level.

monk-extra-attack.name: Extra Attack
monk-extra-attack.summary: When you take the Attack action, you can now make up to 2 attacks within that action.
monk-extra-attack.description:
This progression point changes your basic turn structure by increasing how many attacks fit inside a normal Attack action. It should be reflected everywhere the sheet explains attack cadence.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-stunning-strike.name: Stunning Strike
monk-stunning-strike.summary: Spend focus on a hit to threaten one of the strongest control riders in the class.
monk-stunning-strike.description: When you hit with a melee attack, you can spend 1 Focus Point to force the target to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target is Stunned until the start of your next turn.
monk-stunning-strike.trigger: Immediately after you hit with a melee attack.
monk-stunning-strike.usage: Spend 1 Focus Point per attempted stun.
monk-stunning-strike.effect: On a failed save, the target has the Stunned condition until the start of your next turn.

monk-subclass-feature.name: Subclass Feature
monk-subclass-feature.summary: This level opens or advances your subclass path, adding themed powers unique to your chosen specialization.
monk-subclass-feature.description:
A subclass unlock means the base class starts branching into a more specific identity. The sheet should surface the actual subclass feature rows beside this progression point so the player can tell what changed.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-empowered-strikes.name: Empowered Strikes
monk-empowered-strikes.summary: Your strikes now count as a more reliable supernatural threat against resistant enemies.
monk-empowered-strikes.description:
Empowered Strikes ensures your fists and monk weapon pressure stay relevant when ordinary physical hits would start bouncing off defenses. In play, this should reassure the monk that signature attacks still matter against tougher magical creatures.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-evasion.name: Evasion
monk-evasion.summary: Dexterity-based area threats hurt you less when you react well.
monk-evasion.description:
Evasion turns many reflex-based hazards into manageable or even near-ignored problems. It is a major quality-of-life and survival boost for agile builds.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-stillness-of-mind.name: Stillness of Mind
monk-stillness-of-mind.summary: You can shake off mental disruption and regain control over yourself.
monk-stillness-of-mind.description:
Stillness of Mind is a self-stabilizing tool for moments when fear, charm, or similar effects would otherwise rob the monk of tempo.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-ability-score-improvement-2.name: Ability Score Improvement
monk-ability-score-improvement-2.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
monk-ability-score-improvement-2.description:
Ability Score Improvement is one of the biggest character-shaping progression points. It can either raise the numbers your class depends on most or open a feat that changes what the character can do.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-acrobatic-movement.name: Acrobatic Movement
monk-acrobatic-movement.summary: You move across the battlefield and vertical terrain with fewer normal limitations.
monk-acrobatic-movement.description:
Acrobatic Movement pushes the monk deeper into the role of map-breaker. It matters most on elevation-heavy scenes, chase sequences, rooftops, ledges, and fights where getting to the right square is the whole problem.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-heightened-focus.name: Heightened Focus
monk-heightened-focus.summary: Your focus engine becomes more forgiving, making your best techniques easier to sustain through a long fight.
monk-heightened-focus.description:
Heightened Focus improves the reliability of your core monk resource loop. The player should be able to tell that spending focus is less punishing and that the class can stay online longer across repeated encounters.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-self-restoration.name: Self-Restoration
monk-self-restoration.summary: You can actively clear off problems that would normally keep other front-liners shut down.
monk-self-restoration.description:
Self-Restoration is a quality-of-life and survivability spike. It lets the monk spend internal discipline on fixing conditions or setbacks that would otherwise steal turns, positioning, or momentum.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-subclass-feature-2.name: Subclass Feature
monk-subclass-feature-2.summary: This level opens or advances your subclass path, adding themed powers unique to your chosen specialization.
monk-subclass-feature-2.description:
A subclass unlock means the base class starts branching into a more specific identity. The sheet should surface the actual subclass feature rows beside this progression point so the player can tell what changed.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-ability-score-improvement-3.name: Ability Score Improvement
monk-ability-score-improvement-3.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
monk-ability-score-improvement-3.description:
Ability Score Improvement is one of the biggest character-shaping progression points. It can either raise the numbers your class depends on most or open a feat that changes what the character can do.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-deflect-energy.name: Deflect Energy
monk-deflect-energy.summary: Use your reaction to blunt incoming elemental or force-based punishment the way you already answer weapon pressure.
monk-deflect-energy.description:
Deflect Energy extends the monk’s reactive defense package into magical threats. This matters in caster-heavy encounters where surviving the burst is more important than winning a pure damage race.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.
monk-deflect-energy.trigger: When you take Acid, Cold, Fire, Force, Lightning, or Thunder damage from a visible source.
monk-deflect-energy.effect: Reduce the triggering damage using your Deflect Attacks reduction.

monk-disciplined-survivor.name: Disciplined Survivor
monk-disciplined-survivor.summary: Your body and mind become much harder to crack with failed saves or attrition.
monk-disciplined-survivor.description:
Disciplined Survivor is a late-game reliability feature. It should read like a broad defensive upgrade that makes the monk harder to disable, poison, frighten, or collapse under layered pressure.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-perfect-focus.name: Perfect Focus
monk-perfect-focus.summary: Your focus resource becomes much easier to maintain at the exact point where high-tier encounters start trying to exhaust it.
monk-perfect-focus.description:
Perfect Focus is about consistency. The monk should feel less likely to enter an important scene empty or stay empty after the first explosive exchange.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-ability-score-improvement-4.name: Ability Score Improvement
monk-ability-score-improvement-4.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
monk-ability-score-improvement-4.description:
Ability Score Improvement is one of the biggest character-shaping progression points. It can either raise the numbers your class depends on most or open a feat that changes what the character can do.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-subclass-feature-3.name: Subclass Feature
monk-subclass-feature-3.summary: This level opens or advances your subclass path, adding themed powers unique to your chosen specialization.
monk-subclass-feature-3.description:
A subclass unlock means the base class starts branching into a more specific identity. The sheet should surface the actual subclass feature rows beside this progression point so the player can tell what changed.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-superior-defense.name: Superior Defense
monk-superior-defense.summary: Your defensive tools upgrade into a premium survival mode for the nastiest fights on the table.
monk-superior-defense.description:
Superior Defense is not just another passive line. It is the monk’s statement that, at high level, surviving magical pressure, focused fire, and layered control is part of the class identity.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.
monk-superior-defense.duration: Until the start of your next turn.
monk-superior-defense.usage: Spend 3 Focus Points as a bonus action.
monk-superior-defense.effect: Gain resistance to all damage except Force.

monk-body-and-mind.name: Body and Mind
monk-body-and-mind.summary: Your discipline keeps both your body and your mind steady under pressure.
monk-body-and-mind.description:
Body and Mind is a high-level monk refinement feature. It reinforces the idea that the monk is hard to slow, hard to break, and difficult to throw off balance once fully mastered.

In play, this should feel like calm reliability rather than a tiny background bonus.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-epic-boon.name: Epic Boon
monk-epic-boon.summary: Gain an endgame reward that pushes the build into truly legendary territory.
monk-epic-boon.description:
Epic Boons are late-game progression spikes that add a high-power identity capstone beyond ordinary class scaling.

Think about map pressure, escape lanes, and which target you need to reach this turn.

monk-monk-s-focus-focus-points.name: Monk's Focus (Focus Points)
monk-monk-s-focus-focus-points.summary: Your focus pool fuels the monk’s best mobility, defense, and burst techniques.
monk-monk-s-focus-focus-points.description:
You gain Focus Points, the core resource that powers your monk techniques. Your maximum Focus Points equals your Monk level.

Spend Focus on Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, Step of the Wind, Stunning Strike, and subclass techniques.
monk-monk-s-focus-focus-points.usage: Focus Points equal to your Monk level.
monk-monk-s-focus-focus-points.recovery: Regain all expended Focus Points when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

monk-perfect-discipline.name: Perfect Discipline
monk-perfect-discipline.summary: When initiative starts and you are low on Focus, you regain enough Focus to re-enter your class loop immediately.
monk-perfect-discipline.description: At the start of initiative, if you have fewer than 4 Focus Points, you regain Focus until you have 4. This guarantees your core monk techniques are online at the start of major fights.
monk-perfect-discipline.trigger: At initiative, if you have fewer than 4 Focus Points.
monk-perfect-discipline.usage: Automatic at the start of combat.
monk-perfect-discipline.effect: Restore Focus Points up to 4 when combat starts if below 4.

progressionSummary.summary: Martial Arts, Unarmored Defense, Weapon Mastery (2 weapons).
Monk's Focus (Focus Points), Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, Step of the Wind.

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your attacks, AC, and initiative
buildTips.secondaryReason: improves your AC and monk ability save DC
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: monks are mobile but still need HP to survive focused attacks
